title: Capturing and saving a photo to share
what_it_is: Photo capture means taking a picture of something — like a whiteboard, a handout or a piece of work — using a laptop's Camera app, the Snipping Tool, or a phone camera, so it can be saved and shared digitally.
why_use_it: A quick photo lets you share what happened in a lesson — like notes on a whiteboard — without having to retype or redraw anything.
how_to: On a Windows laptop with a built-in or attached camera: select the Start button (bottom-left of the screen), type Camera, and select the Camera app when it appears.
Point the camera at what you want to capture, then select the round white button on screen to take the photo.
The photo saves automatically. To find it, open File Explorer, then go to Pictures, then Camera Roll.
To capture something already on your screen instead — like a slide or a document — use the Snipping Tool. Select the Start button, type Snipping Tool, and open it.
Select New, then drag a box around the area of the screen you want to capture. The image copies automatically and a preview window opens where you can save it.
On a phone: open the Camera app and take the photo as normal.
If your phone is set up to sync with OneDrive, the photo will upload automatically — open the OneDrive app or website on your laptop to find it under Pictures.
If it isn't syncing automatically, open the Photos app on your phone, select the photo, and use the Share button to send it to yourself by email or Teams.
